name: uma-sdk

metadata.name: uma-sdk
metadata.summary: Python SDK for UMA (universal money address)

documentation:
# uma-python-sdk

The UMA protocol implementation for Python! Check out
the [full documentation](https://docs.uma.me) for more info.

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

modules.name: uma.uma_invoice_creator
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

modules.name: uma.public_key_cache
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

modules.name: uma.protocol.v0.currency
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.currency.Currency.code
documentation:
ISO 4217 currency code (if applicable). For example, USD for US Dollars. For cryptocurrencies, this will
be a ticker symbol, such as BTC for Bitcoin.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.currency.Currency.name
documentation: Full display name of the currency. For example, in USD, the name is "US Dollars".

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.currency.Currency.symbol
documentation: Symbol for this currency. For example, in USD, the symbol is "$".

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.currency.Currency.millisatoshi_per_unit
documentation: Estimated millisats per smallest "unit" of this currency (eg. 1 cent in USD).

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.currency.Currency.min_sendable
documentation:
Minimum amount that can be sent in this currency. This is in the smallest unit of the currency
(eg. cents for USD).

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.currency.Currency.max_sendable
documentation:
Maximum amount that can be sent in this currency. This is in the smallest unit of the currency
(eg. cents for USD).

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.currency.Currency.decimals
documentation:
The number of digits after the decimal point for display on the sender side, and to add clarity
    around what the "smallest unit" of the currency is. For example, in USD, by convention, there are 2 digits for
    cents - $5.95. In this case, `decimals` would be 2. Note that the multiplier is still always in the smallest
    unit (cents). In addition to display purposes, this field can be used to resolve ambiguity in what the multiplier
    means. For example, if the currency is "BTC" and the multiplier is 1000, really we're exchanging in SATs, so
    `decimals` would be 8.
    For details on edge cases and examples, see https://github.com/uma-universal-money-address/protocol/blob/main/umad-04-lnurlp-response.md.

modules.name: uma.version
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

functions.name: uma.version.get_supported_major_versions
documentation:
In the future, we may want to support multiple major versions in the same SDK,
but for now, this keeps things simple.

functions.name: uma.version.get_highest_supported_version_for_major_version
documentation:
Note that this also only supports a single major version for now. If we support
more than one major version in the future, we'll need to change this.

modules.name: uma.protocol.v0.payreq
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.payreq.PayRequest.currency_code
documentation: The currency code for the currency that the receiver will receive for this payment.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.payreq.PayRequest.amount
documentation:
The amount of the payment in the currency specified by `currency_code`. This amount is
in the smallest unit of the specified currency (e.g. cents for USD).

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.payreq.PayRequest.payer_data
documentation:
The data about the payer that the sending VASP must provide in order to send a payment.
This was requested by the receiver in the lnulp response. See LUD-18.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.payreq.PayRequest.requested_payee_data
documentation:
The data about the receiver that the sending VASP would like to know from the receiver.
See LUD-22.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v0.payreq.PayRequest.comment
documentation:
A comment that the sender would like to include with the payment. This can only be included
if the receiver included the `commentAllowed` field in the lnurlp response. The length of
the comment must be less than or equal to the value of `commentAllowed`.

modules.name: uma.protocol.payer_data
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.kyc_status
documentation: KYC information about the sender.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.utxos
documentation: The list of UTXOs of the sender's channels that might be used to forward the payment

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.node_pubkey
documentation: The public key of the sender's node that will be used to send the payment

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.encrypted_travel_rule_info
documentation: The travel rule information of the sender. This is encrypted with the receiver's public encryption key

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.travel_rule_format
documentation:
An optional standardized format of the travel rule information (e.g. IVMS). Null indicates raw json or a custom format.
This field is formatted as <standardized format>@<version> (e.g. ivms@101.2023). Version is optional.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.signature
documentation: Signature is the base64-encoded signature of sha256(ReceiverAddress|Nonce|Timestamp)

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.signature_timestamp
documentation: Time stamp of the signature in seconds

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.utxo_callback
documentation: The URL that the receiver will call to send UTXOs of the channels that receiver used to receive the payment the once it completes.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payer_data.CompliancePayerData.backing_signatures
documentation: List of backing VASP signatures.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.backing_signature.BackingSignature.domain
documentation:
The domain of the backing VASP that produced the signature. Public keys for this VASP will be fetched
    from this domain at /.well-known/lnurlpubkey and used to verify the signature.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.backing_signature.BackingSignature.signature
documentation: Signature of the payload by a backing VASP that can attest to the authenticity of the message.

modules.name: uma.protocol.payee_data
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

classes.name: uma.protocol.pubkey_response.PubkeyResponse

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.pubkey_response.PubkeyResponse.signing_cert_chain
documentation: The certificate chain used to verify signatures from a VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.pubkey_response.PubkeyResponse.encryption_cert_chain
documentation: The certificate chain used to encrypt TR info sent to a VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.pubkey_response.PubkeyResponse.signing_pubkey
documentation: Used to verify signatures from a VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.pubkey_response.PubkeyResponse.encryption_pubkey
documentation: Used to encrypt TR info sent to a VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.pubkey_response.PubkeyResponse.expiration_timestamp
documentation:
Optional expiration_timestamp in seconds since epoch at which these pub keys must be refreshed.
    It can be safely cached until this expiration (or forever if null).

PubkeyResponse is sent from a VASP to another VASP to provide its public keys.
It is the response to GET requests at `/.well-known/lnurlpubkey`.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponseCompliance.utxos
documentation: List of UTXOs of channels over which the receiver will likely receive the payment

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponseCompliance.utxo_callback
documentation: URL that the sender VASP will call to send UTXOs of the channel that the sender used to send the payment once it completes

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponseCompliance.node_pubkey
documentation: Public key of the receiver's node if known

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponseCompliance.signature
documentation:
Signature is the base64-encoded signature of sha256(ReceiverAddress|Nonce|Timestamp)

Note: This field is optional for UMA v0.X backwards-compatibility. It is required for UMA v1.X.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponseCompliance.signature_nonce
documentation:
Random string that is used to prevent replay attacks

Note: This field is optional for UMA v0.X backwards-compatibility. It is required for UMA v1.X.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponseCompliance.signature_timestamp
documentation:
Time stamp of the signature in seconds since epoch

Note: This field is optional for UMA v0.X backwards-compatibility. It is required for UMA v1.X.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponseCompliance.backing_signatures
documentation: List of backing VASP signatures.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponsePaymentInfo.amount
documentation:
The amount that the receiver will receive in the receiving currency not including fees. The amount is specified
in the smallest unit of the currency (eg. cents for USD).

Note: This field is optional for UMA v0.X backwards-compatibility. It is required for UMA v1.X.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponsePaymentInfo.currency_code
documentation: The currency code that the receiver will receive for this payment.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponsePaymentInfo.decimals
documentation:
Number of digits after the decimal point for the receiving currency. For example, in USD, by
convention, there are 2 digits for cents - $5.95. In this case, `decimals` would be 2. This should align with
the currency's `decimals` field in the LNURLP response. It is included here for convenience. See
[UMAD-04](https://github.com/uma-universal-money-address/protocol/blob/main/umad-04-lnurlp-response.md) for
details, edge cases, and examples.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponsePaymentInfo.multiplier
documentation:
The conversion rate. It is the number of millisatoshis that the receiver will receive for 1
unit of the specified currency (eg: cents in USD). In this context, this is just for convenience. The conversion
rate is also baked into the invoice amount itself. Specifically:
`invoiceAmount = amount * multiplier + exchange_fees_msats`

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponsePaymentInfo.exchange_fees_msats
documentation: The fees charged (in millisats) by the receiving VASP for this transaction. This is separate from the `multiplier`.

methods.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponse.append_backing_signature
documentation:
Appends a backing signature to the payreq response.

Args:
    signing_private_key: The private key of the backing VASP which is used to sign the payload.
    domain: The domain of the backing VASP that produced the signature. Public keys for this VASP
    will be fetched from this domain at /.well-known/lnurlpubkey and used to verify the signature.
    sender_address: The sender's UMA address.
    receiver_address: The receiver's UMA address.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponse.encoded_invoice
documentation: The encoded BOLT11 invoice that the sender will use to pay the receiver.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponse.routes
documentation: Always just an empty array for legacy reasons.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponse.payee_data
documentation:
The data about the receiver that the sending VASP requested in the payreq request.
Required for UMA.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponse.payment_info
documentation:
Information about the payment that the receiver will receive. Includes
Final currency-related information for the payment. Required for UMA.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponse.disposable
documentation:
This field may be used by a WALLET to decide whether the initial LNURL link will
be stored locally for later reuse or erased. If disposable is null, it should be
interpreted as true, so if SERVICE intends its LNURL links to be stored it must
return `disposable: false`. UMA should never return `disposable: false` due to
signature nonce checks, etc. See LUD-11.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponse.success_action
documentation: Defines a struct which can be stored and shown to the user on payment success. See LUD-09.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq_response.PayReqResponse.uma_major_version
documentation: The major version of the UMA protocol that this currency adheres to. This is not serialized to JSON.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlComplianceResponse.kyc_status
documentation: Whether the receiver is KYC verified by the receiving VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlComplianceResponse.signature
documentation: DER-encoded signature from the receiving VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlComplianceResponse.signature_nonce
documentation: A random string included in the signature payload to prevent replay attacks.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlComplianceResponse.signature_timestamp
documentation: The time at which the request was made.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlComplianceResponse.is_subject_to_travel_rule
documentation: Whether the receiving VASP is subject to travel rule regulations.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlComplianceResponse.receiver_identifier
documentation: The UMA address of the receiver.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlComplianceResponse.backing_signatures
documentation: List of backing VASP signatures.

methods.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.append_backing_signature
documentation:
Appends a backing signature to the lnurlp response.

Args:
    signing_private_key: The private key of the backing VASP which is used to sign the payload.
    domain: The domain of the backing VASP that produced the signature. Public keys for this VASP
    will be fetched from this domain at /.well-known/lnurlpubkey and used to verify the signature.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.callback
documentation: The URL that the sender will call for the payreq request.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.min_sendable
documentation: The minimum amount that the sender can send in millisatoshis.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.max_sendable
documentation: The maximum amount that the sender can send in millisatoshis.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.encoded_metadata
documentation: JSON-encoded metadata that the sender can use to display information to the user.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.currencies
documentation: The list of currencies that the receiver accepts in order of preference.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.required_payer_data
documentation: The data about the payer that the sending VASP must provide in order to send a payment.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.compliance
documentation: Compliance-related data from the receiving VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.uma_version
documentation: The version of the UMA protocol that the receiver is using.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.comment_chars_allowed
documentation: The number of characters that the sender can include in the comment field of the pay request.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.nostr_pubkey
documentation:
An optional nostr pubkey used for nostr zaps (NIP-57). If set, it should be a valid
BIP-340 public key in hex format.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_response.LnurlpResponse.allows_nostr
documentation: Should be set to true if the receiving VASP allows nostr zaps (NIP-57).

documentation: Symbol for this currency. For example, in USD, the symbol is "$".

documentation: Estimated millisats per smallest "unit" of this currency (eg. 1 cent in USD).

Maximum amount that can be sent in this currency. This is in the smallest unit of the currency

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.currency.Currency.decimals
documentation:
The number of digits after the decimal point for display on the sender side, and to add clarity
    around what the "smallest unit" of the currency is. For example, in USD, by convention, there are 2 digits for
    cents - $5.95. In this case, `decimals` would be 2. Note that the multiplier is still always in the smallest
    unit (cents). In addition to display purposes, this field can be used to resolve ambiguity in what the multiplier
    means. For example, if the currency is "BTC" and the multiplier is 1000, really we're exchanging in SATs, so

    For details on edge cases and examples, see https://github.com/uma-universal-money-address/protocol/blob/main/umad-04-lnurlp-response.md.

documentation:
ISO 4217 currency code (if applicable). For example, USD for US Dollars. For cryptocurrencies, this will
be a ticker symbol, such as BTC for Bitcoin.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.currency.Currency.name

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.currency.Currency.symbol
documentation: Symbol for this currency. For example, in USD, the symbol is "$".

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.currency.Currency.millisatoshi_per_unit
documentation: Estimated millisats per smallest "unit" of this currency (eg. 1 cent in USD).

Minimum amount that can be sent in this currency. This is in the smallest unit of the currency

The number of digits after the decimal point for display on the sender side, and to add clarity
    around what the "smallest unit" of the currency is. For example, in USD, by convention, there are 2 digits for

modules.name: uma.protocol.v1.payreq
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.payreq.PayRequest.sending_amount_currency_code
documentation:
The currency code of the `amount` field. `None` indicates that `amount` is in millisatoshis
as in LNURL without LUD-21. If this is not `None`, then `amount` is in the smallest unit of
the specified currency (e.g. cents for USD). This currency code can be any currency which
the receiver can quote. However, there are two most common scenarios for UMA:

1. If the sender wants the receiver wants to receive a specific amount in their receiving
currency, then this field should be the same as `receiving_currency_code`. This is useful
for cases where the sender wants to ensure that the receiver receives a specific amount
in that destination currency, regardless of the exchange rate, for example, when paying
for some goods or services in a foreign currency.

2. If the sender has a specific amount in their own currency that they would like to send,
then this field should be left as `None` to indicate that the amount is in millisatoshis.
This will lock the sent amount on the sender side, and the receiver will receive the
equivalent amount in their receiving currency. NOTE: In this scenario, the sending VASP
*should not* pass the sending currency code here, as it is not relevant to the receiver.
Rather, by specifying an invoice amount in msats, the sending VASP can ensure that their
user will be sending a fixed amount, regardless of the exchange rate on the receiving side.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.payreq.PayRequest.receiving_currency_code
documentation: The currency code for the currency that the receiver will receive for this payment.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.payreq.PayRequest.amount
documentation:
The amount of the payment in the currency specified by `currency_code`. This amount is
in the smallest unit of the specified currency (e.g. cents for USD).

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.payreq.PayRequest.payer_data
documentation:
The data about the payer that the sending VASP must provide in order to send a payment.
This was requested by the receiver in the lnulp response. See LUD-18.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.payreq.PayRequest.requested_payee_data
documentation:
The data about the receiver that the sending VASP would like to know from the receiver.
See LUD-22.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.v1.payreq.PayRequest.comment
documentation:
A comment that the sender would like to include with the payment. This can only be included
if the receiver included the `commentAllowed` field in the lnurlp response. The length of
the comment must be less than or equal to the value of `commentAllowed`.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.post_tx_callback.PostTransactionCallback.utxos
documentation: List of utxo/amounts corresponding to the VASPs channels.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.post_tx_callback.PostTransactionCallback.vasp_domain
documentation:
The domain of the VASP that is sending the callback. Used by the VASP to fetch the public keys of
its counterparty.

Note: This field is optional for UMA v0.X backwards-compatibility. It is required for UMA v1.X.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.post_tx_callback.PostTransactionCallback.signature
documentation:
Signature is the base64-encoded signature of sha256(Nonce|Timestamp)

Note: This field is optional for UMA v0.X backwards-compatibility. It is required for UMA v1.X.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.post_tx_callback.PostTransactionCallback.signature_nonce
documentation:
Random string that is used to prevent replay attacks

Note: This field is optional for UMA v0.X backwards-compatibility. It is required for UMA v1.X.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.post_tx_callback.PostTransactionCallback.signature_timestamp
documentation:
Time stamp of the signature in seconds since epoch

Note: This field is optional for UMA v0.X backwards-compatibility. It is required for UMA v1.X.

methods.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.append_backing_signature
documentation:
Appends a backing signature to the lnurlp request.

Args:

    domain: The domain of the backing VASP that produced the signature. Public keys for this VASP
    will be fetched from this domain at /.well-known/lnurlpubkey and used to verify the signature.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.receiver_address
documentation: The UMA or Lightning address of the receiver.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.nonce
documentation: A random string included in the signature payload to prevent replay attacks.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.signature
documentation: DER-encoded signature from the sending VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.is_subject_to_travel_rule
documentation: Whether the sending VASP is subject to travel rule regulations.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.vasp_domain
documentation: The domain of the sending VASP.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.timestamp
documentation: The time at which the request was made.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.uma_version

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.lnurlp_request.LnurlpRequest.backing_signatures

modules.name: uma.nonce_cache
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

methods.name: uma.nonce_cache.INonceCache.check_and_save_nonce
documentation:
Checks if the given nonce has been used before, and if not, saves it.
If the nonce has been used before, or if timestamp is too old, raises InvalidNonceException.

Args:
    nonce: the nonce to cache.
    timestamp: timestamp corresponding to the nonce.

methods.name: uma.nonce_cache.INonceCache.purge_nonces_older_than
documentation:
Purges all nonces older than the given timestamp. This allows the cache to be pruned.

Args:
    timestamp: the timestamp before which nonces should be removed.

classes.name: uma.nonce_cache.InMemoryNonceCache
documentation:
InMemoryNonceCache is an in-memory implementation of NonceCache.
It is not recommended to use this in production, as it will not persist across restarts. You likely want to
implement your own NonceCache that persists to a database of some sort.

methods.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.append_backing_signature
documentation:
Appends a backing signature to the payreq request.

Args:
    signing_private_key: The private key of the backing VASP which is used to sign the payload.
    domain: The domain of the backing VASP that produced the signature. Public keys for this VASP
    will be fetched from this domain at /.well-known/lnurlpubkey and used to verify the signature.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.sending_amount_currency_code
documentation:
The currency code of the `amount` field. `None` indicates that `amount` is in millisatoshis
as in LNURL without LUD-21. If this is not `None`, then `amount` is in the smallest unit of
the specified currency (e.g. cents for USD). This currency code can be any currency which
the receiver can quote. However, there are two most common scenarios for UMA:

1. If the sender wants the receiver wants to receive a specific amount in their receiving
currency, then this field should be the same as `receiving_currency_code`. This is useful
for cases where the sender wants to ensure that the receiver receives a specific amount
in that destination currency, regardless of the exchange rate, for example, when paying
for some goods or services in a foreign currency.

2. If the sender has a specific amount in their own currency that they would like to send,
then this field should be left as `None` to indicate that the amount is in millisatoshis.
This will lock the sent amount on the sender side, and the receiver will receive the
equivalent amount in their receiving currency. NOTE: In this scenario, the sending VASP
*should not* pass the sending currency code here, as it is not relevant to the receiver.
Rather, by specifying an invoice amount in msats, the sending VASP can ensure that their
user will be sending a fixed amount, regardless of the exchange rate on the receiving side.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.receiving_currency_code
documentation: The currency code for the currency that the receiver will receive for this payment.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.amount
documentation:
The amount of the payment in the currency specified by `currency_code`. This amount is
in the smallest unit of the specified currency (e.g. cents for USD).

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.payer_data
documentation:
The data about the payer that the sending VASP must provide in order to send a payment.
This was requested by the receiver in the lnulp response. See LUD-18.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.requested_payee_data
documentation:
The data about the receiver that the sending VASP would like to know from the receiver.
See LUD-22.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.comment
documentation:
A comment that the sender would like to include with the payment. This can only be included
if the receiver included the `commentAllowed` field in the lnurlp response. The length of
the comment must be less than or equal to the value of `commentAllowed`.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.uma_major_version
documentation: The major version of the UMA protocol that this currency adheres to. This is not serialized to JSON.

class_variables.name: uma.protocol.payreq.PayRequest.invoice_uuid
documentation: The uma invoice UUID that the sender is paying.

modules.name: uma.uma
documentation: Copyright ©, 2022-present, Lightspark Group, Inc. - All Rights Reserved

functions.name: uma.uma.create_pubkey_response
documentation:
Creates a pubkey response.

Args:
    signing_cert_chain: the chain of signing certificates in PEM format.
        The order of the certificates should be from the leaf to the root.
    encryption_cert_chain: the chain of encryption certificates in PEM format.
        The order of the certificates should be from the leaf to the root.
    expiration_timestamp: the expiration timestamp of the public keys.

functions.name: uma.uma._verify_signature
documentation:
Verifies the signature of the uma request.

Args:
    payload: the raw payload.
    signature: the hex-encoded signature.
    other_vasp_pubkey: the bytes of the signing public key of the VASP who signed the payload.

functions.name: uma.uma.create_pay_request
documentation:
Creates a payreq request object.

Args:
    receiving_currency_code: The code of the currency that the receiver will receive for this
        payment.
    amount: The amount that the receiver will receive in either the smallest unit of the
        receiving currency (if is_amount_in_receiving_currency is True), or in msats (if false).
    is_amount_in_receiving_currency: Whether the amount field is specified in the smallest unit
        of the receiving currency or in msats (if false).
    payer_identifier: The UMA address of the sender. For example, $alice@vasp.com.
    uma_major_version: The major version of the UMA protocol that this currency adheres to.
        If non-UMA, this version is still relevant for which LUD-21 spec to follow. For the older
        LUD-21 spec, this should be 0. For the newer LUD-21 spec, this should be 1.
    payer_name: The name of the sender if requested by the receiver.
    payer_email: The email of the sender if requested by the receiver.
    payer_compliance: The compliance data of the sender. This is REQUIRED for UMA payments,
        but not for regular LNURL payments.
    requested_payee_data: the additional data about the payee which is requested by the sending
        VASP, if any.
    comment: A comment that the sender wants to add to the payment. This can only be included
        if the receiver included the `commentAllowed` field in the lnurlp response. The length of
        the comment must be less than or equal to the value of `commentAllowed`.
    invoice_uuid: if the PayRequest is for an UMA invoice, this should be the UUID of the invoice.

functions.name: uma.uma.create_uma_lnurlp_request_url
documentation:
Creates a signed uma request URL.

Args:
    signing_private_key: the private key of the VASP that is sending the payment. This will be used to sign the request.
    receiver_address: the address of the receiver of the payment (i.e. $bob@vasp2).
    sender_vasp_domain: the domain of the VASP that is sending the payment. It will be used by the receiver to fetch the public keys of the sender.
    is_subject_to_travel_rule: whether the sending VASP is a financial institution that requires travel rule information.

functions.name: uma.uma.verify_uma_lnurlp_query_signature
documentation:
Verifies the primary signature on an uma Lnurlp query based on the public key of the VASP
making the request.

Args:
    request: the signed request to verify.
    other_vasp_signing_pubkey: the public key of the VASP making this request in bytes.

functions.name: uma.uma.verify_uma_lnurlp_query_backing_signatures
documentation:
Verifies the backing signatures on an uma Lnurlp query. You may optionally
call this function after verify_uma_lnurlp_query_signature to verify signatures
from backing VASPs.

Args:
    request: the signed request to verify.
    cache: the public key cache to use. You can use the InMemoryPublicKeyCache class,
    or implement your own persistent cache with any storage type.

functions.name: uma.uma.create_pay_req_response
documentation:
Creates an uma pay request response with an encoded invoice.

Args:
    request: the uma pay request.This will be used to sign the request.
    invoice_creator: the object that will create the invoice. In practice, this is usually a `services.LightsparkClient`.
    metadata: the metadata that will be added to the invoice's metadata hash field.
    receiving_currency_code: the code of the currency that the receiver will receive for this payment. Required for UMA transactions.
    receiving_currency_decimals: the number of decimal places in the specified currency. For example, USD has 2 decimal
        places. This should align with the decimals field returned for the chosen currency in the LNURLP response. Required for UMA transactions.
    msats_per_currency_unit: milli-satoshis per the smallest unit of the specified currency. This rate is committed to by the receiving VASP until the
        invoice expires. Required for UMA transactions.
    receiver_fees_msats: the fees charged (in millisats) by the receiving VASP to convert to the target currency. This is separate from the
        conversion rate. Required for UMA transactions.
    receiver_node_pubkey: the public key of the receiver node.
    utxo_callback: the URL that the receiving VASP will call to send UTXOs of the channel that the receiver used to receive the payment once it completes.
    payee_identifier: the identifier of the receiver. For example, $bob@vasp2.com. Required for UMA transactions.
    signing_private_key: the private key of the VASP that is receiving the payment. This will be used to sign the request. Required for UMA transactions.
    receiver_utxos: the list of UTXOs of the receiver's channels that might be used to fund the payment.
    payee_data: the additional data about the payee which was requested in the pay request by the sending VASP, if any.
    disposable: This field may be used by a WALLET to decide whether the initial LNURL link will be stored locally for later reuse or erased.
        If disposable is null, it should be interpreted as true, so if SERVICE intends its LNURL links to be stored it must return
        `disposable: false`. UMA should never return `disposable: false`. See LUD-11.
    success_action: an action that the wallet should take once the payment is complete. See LUD-09

functions.name: uma.uma.verify_uma_lnurlp_response_backing_signatures
documentation:
Verifies the backing signatures on an uma Lnurlp query. You may optionally
call this function after verify_uma_lnurlp_response_signature to verify signatures
from backing VASPs.

Args:
    response: the signed response to verify.
    cache: the public key cache to use. You can use the InMemoryPublicKeyCache class,
    or implement your own persistent cache with any storage type.

functions.name: uma.uma.create_post_transaction_callback
documentation:
Creates a signed post transaction callback.

Args:
    utxos: UTXOs of the channels of the VASP initiating the callback.
    vasp_domain: the domain of the VASP initiating the callback.
    signing_private_key: the private key of the VASP initiating the callback. This will be used to sign the request.

functions.name: uma.uma.verify_post_transaction_callback_signature
documentation:
Verifies the signature on a post transaction callback based on the public key of the counterparty

Args:
    callback: the signed callback to verify.
    other_vasp_signing_pubkey: the public key of the counterparty VASP.
    nonce_cache: the nonce cache used to prevent replay attacks.

functions.name: uma.uma.create_uma_invoice
documentation:
Creates a UMA invoice.

Args:
    receiver_uma: the UMA address of the receiver.
    receiving_currency_amount: the amount that the receiver will receive in either the smallest unit of the receiving currency.
    receiving_currency: the currency that the receiver will receive for this payment.
    expiration: the expiration time of the invoice in seconds.
    callback: the URL that the receiver will call to send UTXOs of the channel that the receiver used to receive the payment once it completes.
    is_subject_to_travel_rule: whether the receiver is a financial institution that requires travel rule information.
    signing_private_key: the private key of the VASP that is creating the invoice. This will be used to sign the request.
    required_payer_data: RequiredPayerData the data about the payer that the sending VASP must provide in order to send a payment.
    comment_chars_allowed: the number of characters that the receiver allows in the comment field.
    receiver_kyc_status: the KYC status of the receiver.
    invoice_limit: the maximum times of the invoice can be paid.
    sender_uma: the UMA address of the sender.
